commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Anointed, "Christ "as the anointing of kings prefigured that of the Messias, which, in Hebrew, has the same import as the word christos has in Greek. (Calmet)
Wronged. Literally, "by calumny "or by any other mode of oppression. (Haydock)
Despise. Hebrew, "hide my eyes "through confusion. (Calmet)
Protestants, "to blind my eyes therewith. "(Haydock)
Septuagint have read nálim, "shoes "instead of anlim. (Calmet)
"Have I taken from the hand of any one a present, to render me favourable, so much as a shoe? (upodéma, or latchet) answer against me "(Haydock)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
